text:
Since science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) education has become a new pedagogical approach in order to enhance 21st century competencies as well as to encourage innovation, this study aims to analyze the structure of research on STEM and STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, Mathematics) education literacy in Indonesia. By employing systematic literature review (SLR) method with PRISMA guidelines adapted from Sharif and Moher et al. as the basis, this study analyzed 63 articles of science and technology education papers published in 27 journals that are indexed by Scopus and are relevant to STEAM literacy in Indonesia. The study also employed Paper Classification Form (PCF) proposed by Kizilaslan et al. for classification and analysis of the literature. The results are presented by referring to several variables, namely year of publication, research methodology, data collection method, study discipline, as well as the subjects of the research.

This study analyzed scientific literature in Indonesia on research related to students’ scientific learning skills in secondary school and determined research methodologies used and their frequencies. The results show that a high number of studies focus on skills that students learn from secondary school science curriculum. Experimental designs were found as a dominant methodology. Among teaching method approaches, the frequency of occurrence of Project-Based Learning (PjBL), Problem-Based Learning (PBL), and Inquiry-based learning (IBL) were found highest, often combined with STEM or STEAM approaches. Theme analysis revealed that critical thinking skills, scientific literacy, and students’ learning outcomes are major themes that should be strengthened with more robust empirical studies. Ultimately, the study emphasizes the necessity of stronger empirical studies in order to fully realize the promised benefits of STEAM education for the benefit of students to gain 21st century skills.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
